title: eritrichium tenellum
intro: Eritrichium tenellum, commonly known as Needle-and-Thread, is a flowering plant species in the borage family. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antioxidant. The therapeutic actions of Eritrichium tenellum include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. These constituents, including eritrichine, eritrichoside, and eritrichaline, have been found to improve respiratory health by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, making it easier for people to breathe and carry out daily activities. parts: Eritrichium tenellum has many medicinal parts, such as roots, which contain eritrichoside and eritrichoside A, compounds that have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The flowers of Eritrichium tenellum contain sesquiterpene lactones, such as eritrichoside and eritrichoside B, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots and stems also contain iridoid glycosides, including eritrichoside and eritrichoside A, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.

Eritrichium tenellum tea is often used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, due to its carminative properties. A tincture of Eritrichium tenellum, on the other hand, is commonly used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. side_effects: Eritrichium tenellum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in some individuals.
